DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
DAVID CHIU, ESQ. City Attorney, 415) 554-3864 E-Mail: ian.eliasoph@sfcityatty.org for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
Angela Olvera, CSR# 7265, Behmke Reporting & Video Service, 661-587-9040, olveracourtreporting@aol.com, Court Reporter
No other appearances.

Not contested. Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Defendant's "demurrer to plaintiff's claim for damages" is sustained without leave to amend.

Plaintiff is a San Francisco sheriff's deputy who won reinstatement to his job after being fired. Plaintiff then sued defendant alleging FEHA discrimination claims in Case No. CGC-19-581293 for what happened in the reinstatement proceedings. On March 4, 2022, I sustained defendant's demurrer to the third amended complaint (TAC) in case 581293 without leave to amend, finding that plaintiff's "claims are barred by the litigation privilege." That order is incorporated here by reference.

In April 2022, plaintiff filed another FEHA discrimination complaint - Case No. CGC-22-599208 - that largely copies the TAC in Case No. 581293. This complaint continues to allege disputes arising from plaintiff's reinstatement proceedings - this time over back pay, seniority and a "specialty assignment." Defendant again demurs under the litigation privilege. Plaintiff's opposition fails to acknowledge my March 4 order's existence, much less to draw any distinction regarding the litigation privilege between Case Nos. 581293 and 599208. Plaintiff thus effectively concedes that amendment would be fruitless, so the demurrer is sustained without leave.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Angela Olvera, CSR# 7265, Behmke Reporting & Video Service, 661-587-9040, olveracourtreporting@aol.com, Court Reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)
